rationale: The "relaxing puzzle" hook with a hand-on-stylus bus-sorting demo fundamentally mismatches Flip & Dive's core loop, which is about kinetic ragdoll thrills, timing and aerial tricks — not top-down logic puzzles. Visual frictions are significant: the archetype's orange/blue/purple puzzle aesthetic lacks the bright pink character and pool/height-marker signals that make Flip & Dive scroll-stoppable. Audience overlap exists at the broad casual/relaxation level, but adapting this template would require replacing the bus puzzle with a finger-guided dive trajectory plus VO ("ever played a game that actually makes you feel relaxed?") — only worth testing as a low-priority hook variant, not a primary archetype.
